questions.questionText: Write down the number of lines of symmetry of a regular hexagon.
workingSteps: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry: 3 through opposite vertices and 3 through midpoints of opposite sides.
questions.explanation: A regular polygon with n sides has n lines of symmetry.

questions.questionText: Write down the order of rotational symmetry of a rectangle.
workingSteps: A rectangle maps onto itself after rotations of 180° and 360°.
questions.explanation: The order of rotational symmetry is the number of times a shape looks the same in one full turn.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
A shape has exactly 4 lines of symmetry and order of rotational symmetry 4.
Write down the name of one such shape.
workingSteps: A square has 4 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 4.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
A parallelogram (that is not a rectangle) has no lines of symmetry.
Write down the order of rotational symmetry of a parallelogram.
workingSteps: A parallelogram maps onto itself after 180° rotation, so order = 2.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Complete the table by writing the number of lines of symmetry and the order of rotational symmetry for each shape.
workingSteps: Equilateral triangle: 3 lines of symmetry, rotational order 3
Isosceles triangle: 1 line of symmetry, rotational order 1
Rhombus: 2 lines of symmetry, rotational order 2
Regular pentagon: 5 lines of symmetry, rotational order 5
